title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP — Tara Agyemang, Google
summary: WebMCP is a proposed web standard introduced by Google's Chrome team that enables AI agents to interact with websites through structured, named tools instead of parsing DOM and pixel coordinates. It provides both declarative HTML attributes and imperative JavaScript APIs for registering agent-callable actions, demonstrated with a concert ticket purchase flow.
writeup: This talk from Google's Tara Agyemang introduces WebMCP, a proposed web standard from the Chrome team that lets AI agents interact with websites through structured, named tools rather than parsing the DOM and pixel coordinates. WebMCP exposes both declarative HTML attributes and imperative JavaScript APIs for registering agent-callable actions, shown through a concert ticket purchase flow. The approach matters because it shifts browser automation away from brittle screen scraping toward explicit, site-defined actions, with implications for reliability and accessibility.
